exploration_log.aha_moment: The existing MaxDelay cap pattern in RetryHelper.GetRetryDelay uses `if (maxDelay is TimeSpan maxDelayValue && delay > maxDelayValue) return maxDelay.Value`. The same conditional pattern applies directly at the point where `newDelay` is assigned.
exploration_log.hindsight: The fix is a one-line change; reading RetryHelper.GetRetryDelay just confirmed the capping pattern to reuse.

alternatives: Call RetryHelper.GetRetryDelay with newDelay as baseDelay
Use same pattern as RetryHelper: if (maxDelay is TimeSpan maxDelayValue && delay > maxDelayValue)
key_decisions.decision: Apply inline: MaxDelay is TimeSpan maxDelay && newDelay > maxDelay ? maxDelay : newDelay
key_decisions.reasoning: Minimal one-line change that mirrors the existing MaxDelay pattern in RetryHelper, consistent with how the cap is applied elsewhere in the retry codebase.

solve_notes: Located the `DelayGenerator` invocation in `RetryResilienceStrategy.ExecuteCore`. The `delay = newDelay` line assigns the generator's result without any `MaxDelay` cap. Applied the same pattern used in `RetryHelper.GetRetryDelay`: `MaxDelay is TimeSpan maxDelay && newDelay > maxDelay ? maxDelay : newDelay`.
task_text:
Fix RetryResilienceStrategy not capping DelayGenerator results to MaxDelay

`RetryResilienceStrategy.ExecuteCore` applies `MaxDelay` only to the
algorithm-computed delay produced by `RetryHelper.GetRetryDelay`. When
`RetryStrategyOptions.DelayGenerator` is also set and returns a valid
`TimeSpan`, the returned value replaces the computed delay without
being subject to the `MaxDelay` cap. A custom `DelayGenerator`
returning 60 seconds when `MaxDelay` is 5 seconds will proceed
uncapped. Fix `RetryResilienceStrategy.cs` to apply the `MaxDelay`
cap to the delay returned by `DelayGenerator` (using the same
`Math.Min` / `TimeSpan` comparison logic already present in
`RetryHelper.GetRetryDelay`).
